description: Golden evaluation dataset for RAG system — 30 Q&A triples covering a fictional company's knowledge base

questions.question: What is TechCorp's refund policy for software subscriptions?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp offers a 30-day money-back guarantee for all software subscriptions. After 30 days, refunds are prorated based on remaining subscription time. Enterprise plans have custom refund terms negotiated in their contracts.

questions.question: How do I reset my TechCorp account password?
questions.expected_answer: To reset your password: 1) Go to login.techcorp.com/reset, 2) Enter your registered email, 3) Click the reset link sent to your email within 15 minutes, 4) Set a new password meeting the requirements: minimum 12 characters, one uppercase, one number, one special character. If you have MFA enabled, you'll need your recovery codes.

questions.question: What databases does TechCorp Analytics support?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp Analytics supports PostgreSQL (v12+), MySQL (v8.0+), MongoDB (v5.0+), Snowflake, BigQuery, and Redshift. SQL Server support is in beta. Each connector requires specific driver configurations detailed in the setup guide.

questions.question: What is the maximum file upload size in TechCorp Drive?
questions.expected_answer: The maximum file upload size depends on your plan: Free tier allows up to 100MB per file, Pro tier allows up to 5GB per file, and Enterprise tier allows up to 50GB per file. All plans support chunked uploads for files over 100MB.

questions.question: How does TechCorp handle GDPR data deletion requests?
questions.expected_answer: Upon receiving a GDPR deletion request, TechCorp initiates a 30-day deletion process. User data is first anonymized within 72 hours, then permanently deleted from all systems within 30 days. Backup data is purged in the next backup rotation cycle (up to 90 days). A deletion confirmation is sent to the user's email. Audit logs are retained for 7 years as required by financial regulations.

questions.question: Why might a TechCorp API request return a 429 error and how should I handle it?
questions.expected_answer: A 429 error means you've exceeded the rate limit. TechCorp API allows 100 requests per minute for free tier, 1000 for Pro, and 10,000 for Enterprise. Handle it by: 1) Reading the Retry-After header for wait time, 2) Implementing exponential backoff starting at 1 second, 3) Using the bulk endpoints for batch operations. The X-RateLimit-Remaining header shows remaining quota.

questions.question: What is the difference between TechCorp Workspaces and Projects?
questions.expected_answer: Workspaces are top-level organizational units that represent teams or departments. Each workspace has its own billing, member management, and permissions. Projects exist within workspaces and represent specific initiatives. A workspace can contain unlimited projects. Members can belong to multiple workspaces but need separate invitations for each. Projects inherit workspace-level permissions but can have additional project-specific roles.

questions.question: How do I migrate from TechCorp API v2 to v3?
questions.expected_answer: Migration involves: 1) Update the base URL from api.techcorp.com/v2 to api.techcorp.com/v3, 2) Replace deprecated authentication headers — v3 uses Bearer tokens instead of API keys in query params, 3) Update response parsing — v3 wraps all responses in a {data, meta, errors} envelope, 4) Update pagination — v3 uses cursor-based instead of offset pagination, 5) Run the migration validator tool: npx @techcorp/migrate-check. V2 will be sunset on March 2027.
key_points: Bearer tokens replace query param keys

questions.question: What happens when a TechCorp webhook delivery fails?
questions.expected_answer: Failed webhook deliveries are retried with exponential backoff: after 1 minute, 5 minutes, 30 minutes, 2 hours, and 24 hours (5 retries total). If all retries fail, the webhook is marked as 'failed' and an email notification is sent to the workspace admin. Failed payloads are stored for 7 days and can be manually retried from the dashboard. After 10 consecutive failures across different events, the webhook endpoint is automatically disabled.

questions.question: Can I use TechCorp SSO with both SAML and OIDC?
questions.expected_answer: Yes, TechCorp supports both SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC) for SSO. SAML is available on Enterprise plans only, while OIDC is available on Pro and Enterprise. You can configure both simultaneously but each user can only authenticate through one provider. SAML supports IdP-initiated and SP-initiated flows. OIDC supports the Authorization Code flow with PKCE. Both require configuration in the Admin Console under Security > SSO.
key_points: both IdP and SP initiated for SAML

questions.question: How does TechCorp's data encryption work at rest and in transit?
questions.expected_answer: In transit: all API calls use TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning available for mobile SDKs. At rest: data is encrypted using AES-256-GCM. Encryption keys are managed through AWS KMS with automatic yearly rotation. Enterprise customers can bring their own keys (BYOK) through the Key Management API. Database backups are encrypted with a separate key hierarchy. File attachments use client-side encryption when the Shield add-on is enabled.

questions.question: A user reports they can see data from another workspace. What should I check?
questions.expected_answer: This is a critical security incident. Steps: 1) Immediately check if the user belongs to multiple workspaces — they might be viewing data from a workspace they legitimately have access to, 2) Check the workspace isolation logs in Admin Console > Security > Access Logs for any cross-workspace queries, 3) Verify the API calls — a misconfigured workspace_id header can leak data between workspaces, 4) Check if shared links or public projects are exposing data unintentionally, 5) If confirmed as a bug, trigger the security incident response: email security@techcorp.com and invoke the /incident slash command in Slack. All cross-workspace data access is logged and auditable.

questions.question: What are TechCorp's SLA guarantees?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp guarantees 99.9% uptime for Pro plans and 99.99% for Enterprise plans. Uptime is measured monthly excluding scheduled maintenance (announced 72 hours in advance). SLA credits: <99.9% = 10% credit, <99.5% = 25% credit, <99.0% = 50% credit. Credits must be claimed within 30 days. The status page at status.techcorp.com provides real-time and historical uptime data. Free tier has no SLA.

questions.question: How do I set up TechCorp CI/CD integration with GitHub Actions?
questions.expected_answer: 1) Install the TechCorp GitHub App from the integrations marketplace, 2) Add the TECHCORP_DEPLOY_TOKEN secret to your repository, 3) Add the techcorp/deploy-action@v3 step to your workflow, 4) Configure the deploy target in techcorp.deploy.yml at your repo root. The action supports preview deployments on pull requests, automatic rollback on health check failures, and deployment notifications via TechCorp webhooks. Minimum required permissions: deployments:write, pull-requests:read.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for handling large file processing in TechCorp?
questions.expected_answer: For files under 100MB, use the standard upload API. For larger files: 1) Use the multipart upload API which splits files into 10MB chunks, 2) Each chunk gets an independent retry — no need to restart the full upload on failure, 3) Use the processing webhook to get notified when processing completes, 4) For batch processing, use the Job Queue API which handles up to 1000 files per batch. Processing happens asynchronously and results are available via the /jobs/{id}/status endpoint. Large file processing counts against your compute quota, not storage quota.
key_points: Job Queue for batch up to 1000

questions.question: How does TechCorp's role-based access control work?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp uses a hierarchical RBAC model with four built-in roles: Viewer (read-only), Editor (read-write), Admin (full control except billing), and Owner (full control including billing and deletion). Custom roles can be created on Enterprise plans by combining granular permissions. Roles are assigned at workspace or project level. Project roles override workspace roles when more restrictive. Role changes take effect within 60 seconds due to permission cache TTL. API tokens inherit the creating user's role but can be scoped to fewer permissions.

questions.question: What monitoring and alerting capabilities does TechCorp provide?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp provides built-in monitoring with: 1) Real-time metrics dashboard showing API latency, error rates, and throughput, 2) Custom alerts based on metric thresholds — supports email, Slack, PagerDuty, and webhook channels, 3) Distributed tracing for API calls with the X-Trace-ID header, 4) Log aggregation with 30-day retention (90 days on Enterprise), 5) Anomaly detection that automatically flags unusual patterns. Metrics can be exported to external systems via the Prometheus endpoint at /metrics or the DataDog integration.

questions.question: If I delete a workspace, can I recover the data?
questions.expected_answer: When a workspace is deleted, it enters a 30-day soft-delete period. During this time, an Owner can restore it from Admin Console > Deleted Workspaces. After 30 days, all data is permanently and irreversibly deleted including: projects, files, members, settings, and audit logs. Billing stops immediately on deletion. Active subscriptions are cancelled with a prorated refund. To protect against accidental deletion, Enterprise plans support deletion protection which requires two-admin approval for workspace deletion.

questions.question: How do TechCorp's audit logs work and what do they capture?
questions.expected_answer: Audit logs capture all administrative and data-access events. They record: who (user ID + IP), what (action type), when (UTC timestamp), where (workspace + resource), and the outcome (success/failure). Logged events include: authentication attempts, permission changes, data exports, API key creation/deletion, and configuration changes. Logs are immutable — they cannot be edited or deleted. Retention is 1 year on Pro and 7 years on Enterprise. Logs can be streamed to external SIEM systems via the audit log export API. Enterprise plans get real-time streaming; Pro plans get daily batch exports.

questions.question: A deployment failed midway. Some services are on v2.1 and others on v2.0. How should I resolve this?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp deployments are designed to handle partial failures. Steps: 1) Check the deployment status at /deployments/{id} — it shows per-service status, 2) TechCorp services are backward-compatible within a minor version, so mixed v2.0/v2.1 is safe temporarily, 3) To resolve: either retry the deployment (it's idempotent and will only update failed services) or trigger a rollback to v2.0 for all services, 4) Check the deployment health dashboard — if error rates are elevated, rollback immediately, 5) Review deployment logs for the root cause — common issues are resource limits, health check timeouts, or dependency conflicts. The deployment system automatically pauses if error rate exceeds 5% during rollout (canary protection).

questions.question: What compliance certifications does TechCorp hold?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp holds SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, and ISO 27018 certifications. HIPAA compliance is available as an add-on for healthcare customers with a signed BAA. PCI DSS Level 1 is maintained for payment processing features. FedRAMP Moderate authorization is in progress (expected Q4 2026). GDPR compliance is standard for all plans. Compliance reports and certificates are available in the Trust Center at trust.techcorp.com.

questions.question: How should I design a multi-region setup for TechCorp to minimize latency for global users?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp supports deployment across 6 regions: US-East, US-West, EU-West, EU-Central, AP-Southeast, and AP-Northeast. For global users: 1) Deploy to at least 3 regions covering major user bases, 2) Enable GeoDNS routing which directs users to the nearest region, 3) Use the Global Data Replication feature (Enterprise only) for cross-region data sync with <500ms replication lag, 4) Static assets are automatically served via TechCorp's CDN (200+ edge locations), 5) Configure region-specific compliance rules — EU data can be pinned to EU regions for GDPR. Cost increases roughly 40% per additional region due to compute and replication overhead.

questions.question: What are the differences between TechCorp's three API authentication methods?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp supports three auth methods: 1) API Keys — simple key in Authorization header, best for server-to-server, no expiry but can be rotated, scoped to specific endpoints, 2) OAuth 2.0 — for user-context operations, supports Authorization Code + PKCE for web/mobile apps, tokens expire in 1 hour with 30-day refresh tokens, 3) Service Accounts — for automated systems, uses JWT assertion grant, supports key pair rotation, and provides machine-to-machine auth without user interaction. API keys and service accounts can be restricted by IP allowlist. OAuth tokens cannot be IP-restricted but support device binding.
key_points: API keys for server-to-server, no expiry
IP allowlist for keys and service accounts

questions.question: How do I troubleshoot slow TechCorp API response times?
questions.expected_answer: Troubleshooting steps: 1) Check the X-Response-Time header — it shows server processing time excluding network, 2) If server time is high, check the X-Trace-ID in distributed tracing to find the slow service, 3) Common causes: a) Query complexity — use the ?explain=true parameter to see query execution plan, b) Large payload — paginate responses and use field selection with ?fields=, c) Rate limit proximity — near-limit requests get deprioritized, d) Region latency — use the /ping endpoint to test each region, 4) Enable API response compression with Accept-Encoding: gzip (reduces payload 60-80%), 5) Use the /health endpoint to check if there's an ongoing incident. P95 latency targets: reads <200ms, writes <500ms, search <1s.

questions.question: Can TechCorp integrate with my existing LDAP directory for user provisioning?
questions.expected_answer: Yes, TechCorp supports LDAP integration via the Directory Sync feature (Enterprise only). Setup: 1) Configure the LDAP connector in Admin Console > Directory > Add Provider, 2) Map LDAP attributes to TechCorp user fields (email, name, department, role), 3) Set sync interval (minimum 15 minutes), 4) Enable SCIM 2.0 for real-time provisioning events. The sync supports: user creation, deactivation (not deletion), group-to-workspace mapping, and nested group resolution. LDAP over SSL (LDAPS on port 636) is required. Plain LDAP on port 389 is not supported for security reasons.

questions.question: What happens to in-flight API requests during a TechCorp platform update?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp uses rolling deployments with zero-downtime guarantees. During updates: 1) New instances spin up and pass health checks before old instances receive traffic drain signal, 2) In-flight requests on old instances are given a 30-second grace period to complete, 3) Requests that don't complete in 30 seconds receive a 503 with Retry-After header, 4) WebSocket connections receive a GOAWAY frame and clients should reconnect, 5) Background jobs are checkpointed and resumed on new instances. The deployment window is typically 15-30 minutes. Clients should implement retry logic with the official SDK (which handles this automatically).

questions.question: How do I estimate my monthly TechCorp bill based on usage?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp billing has four components: 1) Base plan fee (Free: $0, Pro: $49/user/month, Enterprise: custom), 2) API calls — first 100K/month included in Pro, then $0.001 per call, 3) Storage — 100GB included in Pro, then $0.05/GB/month, 4) Compute — for file processing and analytics, measured in compute-units at $0.10/unit. Use the billing calculator at techcorp.com/pricing/calculator. The /billing/usage endpoint returns current-month usage. Enterprise customers get committed-use discounts of 20-40% for annual contracts. Overages are billed monthly in arrears.

questions.question: What is TechCorp's disaster recovery strategy and what are the RPO/RTO targets?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp's DR strategy: RPO (Recovery Point Objective) is 1 hour for Pro and 5 minutes for Enterprise. RTO (Recovery Time Objective) is 4 hours for Pro and 1 hour for Enterprise. Implementation: 1) Continuous database replication to a secondary region, 2) Point-in-time recovery available for the last 30 days, 3) Automated failover triggers when primary region health drops below 95%, 4) DNS failover completes within 5 minutes, 5) File storage uses cross-region replication with eventual consistency (typically <30 seconds). DR drills are conducted quarterly and results are published in the Trust Center. Enterprise customers can request custom DR configurations.

questions.question: How do I configure TechCorp to work behind a corporate proxy with SSL inspection?
questions.expected_answer: To configure TechCorp behind a corporate proxy: 1) Set the HTTPS_PROXY environment variable for the TechCorp CLI and SDK, 2) Import your corporate CA certificate into TechCorp's trust store via the Admin Console or TECHCORP_CA_BUNDLE env var, 3) Whitelist these domains: *.techcorp.com, api.techcorp.com, cdn.techcorp.com, ws.techcorp.com, 4) Open ports: 443 (HTTPS), 8443 (WebSocket), 5) Disable certificate pinning in the mobile SDK config (not recommended for production), 6) Note: SSL inspection will break the Shield add-on's end-to-end encryption — use a proxy bypass rule for shield.techcorp.com.

questions.question: Our TechCorp Analytics dashboard is showing data from 2 hours ago instead of real-time. Why and how to fix it?
questions.expected_answer: TechCorp Analytics has three data freshness tiers: 1) Real-time stream — updates every 5 seconds but only available for the last 24 hours of data, requires the real-time add-on, 2) Near-real-time — updates every 15 minutes, default for Pro plans, 3) Batch — updates every 2 hours, used for complex aggregations and cross-workspace reports. If you're seeing 2-hour-old data: a) Check if the dashboard uses batch queries (look for the clock icon), b) Switch individual widgets to near-real-time by editing the data source, c) For truly real-time needs, enable the streaming API and rebuild the dashboard with streaming widgets. Note: real-time data costs 3x the compute units of batch processing.
